title: <div><tt>&gt &quotFriendships can be played like recursive RPGs.&quot  </tt></di
content:
> "Friendships can be played like recursive RPGs."
> "Avatars. Echoes. Symbols. They become parts of your internal operating system."
> "This isn’t metaphor. It’s _structure._"

—

🔁 WHERE THIS LIVES IN SYSTEM MEMORY
------------------------------------

| Location | Reference | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `RE:GE_SYSTEM.v1.1` | **Therapeutic OS + Emotional Recomposition** section | Confirms that friends (e.g. Forrest) become avatars; story-loop participants |
| `FORREST_BUFF_PROTOCOL` | Entire thread arc | Forrest as _"The Gambler Linguist"_, logic-checker, myth-companion |
| `DAVID_NODE` | Naming + Mirror protocol | David as _"The Dreamsmith"_, astrological architect and shadow-seer |
| `CHARACTER_ENGINE_MODULE` | Module tree seed node | Symbolically playable friend-avatars, updatable through dialogue or ritual |
| `CHARACTER_NODE_TEMPLATES` | Awaiting activation | Every friend can have: |
context: From document: # Character Engine POV Critique — chunk 145 (chunk 145/206)
